title: OECD Pillar Two - Global Minimum Tax Taking action: managing your compliance obligations - Deloitte
summary: OECD Pillar Two - Global Minimum Tax Taking action: managing your compliance obligations Deloitte
ai_summary: This item discusses the practical challenges and compliance obligations for multinational enterprises arising from the OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax rules. It emphasizes the need for proactive measures to manage the complex reporting and tax calculations required by the GloBE rules.

title: The future of tax incentives in the GCC after pillar two - International Tax Review
summary: The future of tax incentives in the GCC after pillar two International Tax Review

It critically examines how these global minimum tax rules will necessitate a re-evaluation and potential restructuring of national and regional investment promotion strategies.

ai_summary: This article examines the practical administrative challenges and complexities faced by multinational enterprises during the inaugural filing season for the OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax rules. It highlights the gap between the theoretical framework of the GloBE rules and the operational realities of compliance for businesses worldwide.

title: What does Kuwait’s OECD QDMTT recognition mean for multinationals? - Kuwait Times
summary: What does Kuwait’s OECD QDMTT recognition mean for multinationals? Kuwait Times
ai_summary: Kuwait has received OECD recognition for its Qualified Domestic Minimum Top-up Tax (QDMTT), signaling a significant step towards its Pillar Two implementation. This recognition impacts multinational enterprises operating in Kuwait, requiring them to factor Kuwait's QDMTT into their global minimum tax calculations and potentially reducing the top-up tax payable in other jurisdictions under the Income Inclusion Rule (IIR) or Undertaxed Profits Rule (UTPR).

title: Global Minimum Tax Creates Dispute Risks, Polish Official Says
summary: Global Minimum Tax Creates Dispute Risks, Polish Official Says news.bloombergtax.com

title: Proposed Directive to implement Pillar Two in the EU - KPMG

title: Finally, the European Court of Justice cracks down on trusts
summary: The Tax Justice Network has banged the drum about the threats posed by trusts for decades. On 21 May 2026, the European Court of Justice has finally joined us. ai_summary: The European Court of Justice issued significant rulings concerning trusts, directly addressing risks related to secrecy and asset protection. These judgments represent a landmark development in combating the misuse of trusts for tax avoidance and promoting greater transparency within the EU's financial systems.
